alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has designed and directed LLM coding agents to build production systems and owns evaluation methodology and benchmarking—not architecture design, model training, or scaling LLM training runs.
alignments.job_requirement: Design, prototype and scale up new architectures to improve model intelligence

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has optimization and debugging experience from commercial operations and infrastructure work; no evidence of model-level or computational performance optimization for LLM training.
alignments.job_requirement: Study, debug, and optimize both model performance and computational performance

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has led transformation programs and cross-functional experiments in commercial operations and product; research-level experimental rigor and instrumentation are not evidenced.
alignments.job_requirement: Execute and analyze experiments autonomously and collaboratively

alignments.candidate_evidence: No evidence of contributions to LLM training runs, model checkpoints, or training infrastructure.
alignments.job_requirement: Have experience landing contributions to major LLM training runs

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has basic Python, learning data analysis, and AI/agentic tooling design direction; no evidence of transformer architecture knowledge or efficiency optimizations.
alignments.job_requirement: Well-versed in the state of the art transformer modifications for efficiency

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has built Looker dashboards and managed Salesforce administration; no evidence of infrastructure contribution or systems-level engineering.
alignments.job_requirement: Contribute to training and inference infrastructure

gaps.gap: No background in deep learning, model training, or LLM architecture design
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require fundamental retraining in neural-network architectures, transformer models, and training dynamics—outside the scope of onboarding to a research-level role.

gaps.gap: No hands-on experience with major LLM training runs or model checkpoints
gaps.mitigation: This is a core requirement and a disqualifying gap; no equivalent commercial operations experience translates.

gaps.gap: Python and ML tooling at learning/basic level, not production research depth
gaps.mitigation: Role requires sophisticated empirical debugging and architecture iteration; basic Python is insufficient for research-level LLM work.

gaps.gap: No research publication record, peer-review experience, or academic rigor signals
gaps.mitigation: Research roles typically prioritize research credentials; candidate profile does not mention them.

hard_blockers.evidence: JD states 'you will push the frontier of LLM development' and candidates 'have experience landing contributions to major LLM training runs' with 'deep understanding of LLM architectures, a sophisticated understanding of model inference, and a hands-on empirical approach.' Candidate has no training-run, architecture-design, or inference-optimization background; background is commercial operations and product transformation, not deep learning research.

output.level_rationale: Role requires deep LLM research and model-training expertise, hands-on architecture design and optimization, autonomous empirical work, and landing major contributions to LLM training runs. These are L6-equivalent research-track competencies at a frontier AI organization. JD does not state years of experience, team size, or reporting line. London-based hybrid research role at OpenAI implies senior IC or early-senior researcher scope.
output.summary: This is a frontier LLM research role requiring deep knowledge of transformer architectures, hands-on contributions to major model-training runs, and autonomous empirical debugging of training and inference performance. The candidate's eight years of experience in Strategy and Operations, Commercial Operations, Product Transformation, and AI/agentic tooling *direction* are fundamentally misaligned with research-track deep learning and model-training depth. While the candidate can design and direct LLM systems and has strong operational and strategic capabilities, the role is disqualifying for lack of LLM-training background, architecture expertise, and production research credibility. This is not a bridge or stretch fit; it is a different function family entirely.
uncertainties: JD does not specify years of experience or research publication expectations; confidence in level is high but not absolute.
No salary range or exact reporting line provided in JD.

Unclear whether candidate's 'AI and agentic tooling' work involved any hands-on model-training or infrastructure contribution (beyond direction), which might marginally shift evidence on one alignment.
